subsection.heading: Board of Trustees.—
subsection.content: A self-perpetuating board of trustees shall manage and direct the property and affairs of the corporation.

subsection.heading: Powers.—
subsection.chapeau: The board of trustees may adopt and amend a constitution, bylaws, and regulations for—

paragraph.content: the conduct of the affairs of the corporation.

html:tr.style: font-size:6pt; border-bottom:1px solid black; border-top:1px solid black; -uslm-lc:h1;

html:th.style: min-width: 44.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-right:1px solid black;
width:68.0pt ; max-width:68.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-right:1px solid black; border-left:1px solid black;
width:94.0pt ; max-width:94.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-left:1px solid black;

html:td.style: text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-right:1px solid black; padding-right:2pt;
text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-right:1px solid black; border-left:1px solid black; padding-right:2pt; padding-left: 2pt;
html:td.html:p: 36:4704 (1st par., last par. less regulations for membership).

html:td.style: text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-left:1px solid black; padding-left: 2pt;
html:td.html:p: May 31, 1920, ch. 216, § 4 (1st par., last par. less regulations for membership), 41 Stat. 693.

note.p: The text of 36:4704 (2d sentence) is omitted as executed and obsolete.

In subsection (b), before clause (1), the words “adopt and amend” are substituted for “adopt from time to time . . . and alter, amend, or repeal” for consistency in the revised title and to eliminate unnecessary words. The word “rules” is omitted as included in “regulations”.
